skill.name: developing-genkit-python
skill.description: Develop AI-powered applications using Genkit in Python. Use when the user asks about Genkit, AI agents, flows, or tools in Python, or when encountering Genkit errors, import issues, or API problems.
skill.summary: Guide for building AI applications with Google Genkit in Python
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 233 potential issues, but all are false positives from pattern-matching markdown documentation. The skill is a Firebase-authored Genkit Python development guide that provides legitimate shell commands for development workflows (uv package manager, genkit CLI), references Google AI API endpoints, and instructs developers to configure their own GEMINI_API_KEY. No malicious code patterns confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Documentation
medium_findings.description: Markdown contains shell commands (uv add, genkit start, npm install) documented for developer guidance. These are legitimate development workflow instructions from an official Firebase skill, not malicious execution patterns.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All shell commands are markdown code blocks documenting standard Python/Node.js installation workflows. No actual code execution occurs - these are documentation examples.

medium_findings.title: API Key Configuration Instructions
medium_findings.description: Skill instructs developers to set GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable. This is expected pattern for AI development skills - developers configure their own credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Placeholder patterns ('your-api-key-here') indicate documentation, not credential harvesting. This is standard practice for development skill documentation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation references URLs like Google AI Studio API key page and localhost development endpoints. These are legitimate documentation links.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are standard documentation references to official Google services (aistudio.google.com) and local development endpoints (localhost:4000).

content.user_title: Build AI apps with Google Genkit in Python
content.value_statement: Building AI-powered applications with Google Genkit in Python requires understanding flows, tools, and streaming patterns. This skill provides structured guidance for Genkit Python development.
actual_capabilities: Generate code for AI-powered applications using Genkit in Python
Debug Genkit import errors and common Python API issues
Create AI flows with structured output using Pydantic schemas
Set up Genkit with FastAPI for HTTP serving
Create AI tools with proper parameter schemas for Gemini
limitations: Requires external GEMINI_API_KEY for Google AI integration
Does not execute code or run genkit start - provides guidance only
Does not include runtime environment or package installation

use_cases.title: Building AI-powered Python applications
use_cases.description: Developers building new AI features can use this skill to scaffold Genkit applications with proper Python patterns, flows, and Google AI integration.

use_cases.title: Debugging Genkit errors and import issues
use_cases.description: When encountering Genkit Python errors, import issues, or API problems, this skill provides step-by-step troubleshooting guidance.

use_cases.title: Integrating Genkit with FastAPI services
use_cases.description: Backend developers can use this skill to integrate Genkit flows into FastAPI applications with proper streaming and parallel execution.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers adding AI to web services

prompt_templates.title: Basic Genkit setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new Genkit Python project with Google AI. I want to use Python 3.14 with uv for dependency management.

prompt_templates.title: Create an AI flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Genkit flow that takes a URL as input and returns a summary. Use Pydantic for input schema and streaming responses.

prompt_templates.title: Debug import errors
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm getting 'ModuleNotFoundError: No module named genkit' when running my Genkit app. How do I fix this?

prompt_templates.title: Set up FastAPI integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a FastAPI endpoint that calls a Genkit flow with streaming support. Show me the decorator order and SSE configuration.

output_examples.input: Help me create a simple Genkit flow that generates a joke
output_examples.output: Created a Genkit Python application with a joke_generator flow. Key files: src/main.py with async main(), using ai.run_main(). Configure GEMINI_API_KEY, then run: genkit start -- uv run src/main.py

output_examples.input: I'm getting 'AttributeError: Genkit object has no attribute define_tool'
output_examples.output: Use @ai.tool() decorator instead of @ai.define_tool(). Tools must accept a Pydantic BaseModel as input. Example: class WeatherInput(BaseModel): city: str @ai.tool() async def get_weather(input: WeatherInput)

output_examples.input: How do I set up streaming with FastAPI?
output_examples.output: Use genkit_fastapi_handler decorator on your flow. Flow must accept ctx: ActionRunContext and call ctx.send_chunk(). Client sends Accept: text/event-stream header. Decorator order: @app.post then @genkit_fastapi_handler(ai) then @ai.flow()

best_practices: Always use Pydantic BaseModel for tool and flow input schemas - Gemini requires object parameters
Use ai.run_main(main()) instead of asyncio.run() for apps started with genkit start
Always prefix model IDs with plugin name: googleai/gemini-flash-latest not just gemini-flash-latest
anti_patterns: Do not use bare scalar parameters in tools - always wrap in Pydantic BaseModel
Do not use asyncio.run() for long-lived servers - use ai.run_main(main()) instead
Do not skip plugin prefix on model IDs - will cause model not found errors

faq.question: What Python version is required for Genkit?
faq.answer: Genkit Python requires Python 3.14 or higher. Always use a virtual environment with uv.

faq.question: How do I get started with Google AI in Genkit?
faq.answer: Install genkit and genkit-plugin-google-genai with uv. Set GEMINI_API_KEY in your environment. Use model='googleai/gemini-flash-latest'.

faq.question: Why am I getting ModuleNotFoundError for genkit?
faq.answer: Run 'uv add genkit genkit-plugin-google-genai' to install the required packages in your project.

faq.question: How do I debug flow issues in the Dev UI?
faq.answer: Run 'genkit start -- uv run src/main.py' then open http://localhost:4000. Use the Traces panel to inspect each step.

faq.question: Why does my streaming not work with FastAPI?
faq.answer: Ensure your flow accepts ctx: ActionRunContext and calls ctx.send_chunk(). Client must send Accept: text/event-stream header.

faq.question: What is the correct entrypoint for Genkit apps?
faq.answer: Use ai.run_main(main()) for apps started with genkit start. Use asyncio.run(main()) only for one-off scripts that exit immediately.
